profile.vibe: Cultural immersion, themed trails, grazing, oddities

trails.name: Dumpling Universe
trails.description: Every dumpling tradition: XLB, pierogi, momos, mandu, gyoza, varenyky

trails.name: Noodle Run
trails.description: Pho, ramen, hand-pulled, khao soi, naengmyeon, fresh pasta

trails.name: Sweet Tooth Mega
trails.description: Doughnuts, baklava, gelato, mochi, churros, Indian sweets, ube

trails.name: Fire Walk
trails.description: Spiciest dish at every stop — a heat ladder from warm to nuclear

trails.name: Fermentation Station
trails.description: Kimchi, kombucha, pickles, funky cheese, fermented hot sauce

trails.name: By Hand
trails.description: Foods eaten with your hands: injera, KBBQ wraps, birria, dim sum, elote

trails.name: Curiosity Cabinet
trails.description: Oddities, antiques, weird museums, taxidermy, outsider art

trails.name: Snack Attack
trails.description: Exotic snacks, international groceries, pick-n-mix, global menu

trails.name: Trust the Chef
trails.description: Let the kitchen decide — omakase mentality, tasting menus, chef's picks

trails.name: Weekend Warriors
trails.description: Weekend-only specials, Sunday markets, Saturday brunch rituals

trails.name: Free & Outside
trails.description: Murals, trails, conservatories, wildlife, walks — all free

places.name: Kasama
places.must_try: Ube pancakes at brunch, or the full prix fixe dinner
places.description: World's only Michelin-starred Filipino restaurant. Brunch is the accessible entry — ube pancakes, longanisa, lumpia. Dinner is a prix fixe revelation.
places.rationale: THE reason Chicago's Filipino scene is having a moment. Nothing like this exists anywhere else on Earth.

places.name: Galit
places.must_try: Duck kibbeh and the wood-fired pita with dips
places.description: Michelin-starred modern Middle Eastern. Wood-fired pita, lamb shawarma, beet hummus, duck kibbeh.
places.rationale: Your splurge meal. Trust the chef completely — every dish reinvents a classic.

places.name: HaiSous Vietnamese Kitchen
places.must_try: Whole roasted catfish or the bo luc lac (shaking beef)
places.description: Bib Gourmand Vietnamese that goes way beyond pho. Bold, precise flavors cooked for a Vietnamese audience.
places.rationale: Trust-the-kitchen dream. Pairs perfectly with a Pilsen mural walk.

places.name: Birrieria Zaragoza
places.must_try: Lamb birria with consomé — dip the taco, sip the broth
places.description: Bib Gourmand birria specialist. One family, one dish, perfected over decades.
places.rationale: Food writers fly to Chicago for this. Weekend mornings are peak.

places.name: Demera Ethiopian
places.must_try: Demera Sampler platter — communal injera spread with 5+ stews, plus the traditional coffee ceremony
places.description: Full-sensory Ethiopian dining. Everything on a shared injera platter — tear off spongy bread and scoop stews with your hands. The coffee ceremony is a ritual: beans roasted tableside, ground fresh, brewed in a jebena. It's theater, flavor, and togetherness.
places.rationale: Ethiopian dining is a sensory experience unlike anything else — textures, communal eating, the aromas of the coffee ceremony. Near Argyle Street for a neighborhood double feature.

places.name: Sabri Nihari
places.must_try: Nihari — slow-braised beef shank, bone marrow melting into spiced gravy
places.description: Bib Gourmand Pakistani. The nihari might be the best $12 dish in Chicago.
places.rationale: Bib Gourmand at street-food prices. Devon Avenue itself is the experience.

places.name: The Gundis Kurdish Kitchen
places.must_try: Weekend brunch — the full Kurdish breakfast spread
places.description: One of very few Kurdish restaurants in America. Michelin-recommended. Owners fled Iraq.
places.rationale: Kurdish food doesn't exist outside major metros. The story is as powerful as the food.

places.name: Ghin Khao Eat Rice
places.must_try: Larb with toasted rice powder — max spice
places.description: Bib Gourmand Isan Thai. Not your pad thai shop. The chef doesn't dumb it down.
places.rationale: Regional Isan Thai most Americans have never tasted. The spice is REAL.

places.name: MCCB (Mala Chinese BBQ)
places.must_try: Whole fish in mala chili oil — the numbness is the point
places.description: Szechuan peppercorns will rewire your taste buds. Dry pot and BBQ skewers equally insane.
places.rationale: Your burn-it-down spice tolerance meets its match here.

places.name: Maman Zari
places.must_try: The saffron rice — you eat what Maman makes tonight
places.description: One of very few Persian tasting-menu restaurants in the country. 9 courses, family-run, intimate.
places.rationale: Chef's-pick ethos IS the format. Ultra-rare cuisine at a fraction of fine-dining.

places.name: San Soo Gab San
places.must_try: Galbi over charcoal + wrap in lettuce with ssamjang
places.description: Old-school KBBQ with charcoal grills, 25+ banchan. BYOB. Cook your own.
places.rationale: Interactive dining that IS the activity. Your sister already loves KBBQ.

places.name: Iron Age Korean Steakhouse
places.must_try: All-you-can-eat — try the spicy chicken and unmarinated pork belly
places.description: AYCE Korean BBQ for ~$29. Tabletop grilling, full DIY experience.
places.rationale: She loved KBBQ — this is the unlimited version. Also has a Glenview location.

places.name: Hing Kee Restaurant
places.must_try: Crab and pork xiao long bao — best soup dumplings in Chicago
places.description: Best soup dumplings in the city. Three varieties, delicate wrappers, BYOB.
places.rationale: Dumpling Universe essential. The XLB are why Chinatown exists.

places.name: QXY Dumplings
places.must_try: Lamb and coriander dumplings — hand-folded to order
places.description: Minimalist BYOB with creative fillings: lamb, crab roe, sea urchin. Every dumpling fresh.
places.rationale: The artisan counterpoint to Hing Kee. Creative flavors you won't find elsewhere.

places.name: MingHin Cuisine
places.must_try: Creamy egg yolk buns (liu sha bao) — lava center
places.description: Bib Gourmand Cantonese. Outstanding dim sum, open until midnight.
places.rationale: Late-night dim sum option and the egg yolk buns are a sweet-tooth essential.

places.name: Phoenix Restaurant
places.must_try: Har gow from the cart + chicken feet if you dare
places.description: Classic cart-service dim sum — point at what looks good from rolling carts.
places.rationale: Cart-service is a dying art. Weekend mornings are the move.

places.name: Potsticker House
places.must_try: Pan-fried pork potstickers — crispy bottom, juicy inside
places.description: Name says it all. Decades of perfecting one thing.
places.rationale: Dumpling Universe essential — the potsticker specialists.

places.name: Kasia's Polish Deli
places.must_try: Potato and cheese pierogi with fried onions
places.description: 4x 'Best Pierogi in Chicago.' Hand-pinched, counter-service, grandmother's kitchen.
places.rationale: Chicago has the largest Polish population outside Warsaw. This is the distillation.

places.name: Pierogi Kitchen
places.must_try: Smoked brisket pierogi — Polish meets Texas
places.description: Creative combos like smoked brisket alongside traditional potato-cheese. Polish folk art decor.
places.rationale: The modern counterpoint to Kasia's — creative fillings, same soul.

places.name: Tryzub Ukrainian Kitchen
places.must_try: Varenyky party platter (16 dumplings, mixed fillings)
places.description: Handmade varenyky, borscht, kovbasa. The Ukrainian counterpart to pierogi.
places.rationale: Dumpling Universe essential — Ukrainian dumplings a block from Polish pierogi. Compare!

places.name: The Momo World
places.must_try: Jhol momo — dumplings swimming in spiced tomato broth
places.description: Dedicated momo shop on Devon Ave. Steamed, fried, jhol (soup) styles.
places.rationale: Nepali momos complete the Dumpling Universe. Pair with Sabri Nihari on Devon.

places.name: Tortello
places.must_try: Whatever filled pasta they made today — it changes daily
places.description: Pastificio (pasta factory) with daily-changing fresh filled pastas. Counter-service, watch them make it.
places.rationale: Dumpling Universe: Italian edition. Fresh tortellini/ravioli made before your eyes.

places.name: Ramen Wasabi
places.must_try: Tonkotsu ramen — 45-hour pork bone broth
places.description: 45-hour broth. Creamy, rich, deeply savory tonkotsu. Cash only.
places.rationale: Noodle Run essential. The broth time alone tells you this is serious.

places.name: High Five Ramen
places.must_try: Hidden basement ramen — follow the signs downstairs
places.description: Hidden in a basement beneath Green Street Smoked Meats. The secret entrance is part of the fun.
places.rationale: Noodle Run + Curiosity Cabinet crossover. Secret ramen in a basement.

places.name: Slurp Slurp Noodles
places.must_try: Watch them pull the noodles, then eat the biang biang belt noodles
places.description: Hand-pulled noodles made in front of you. Wide belt noodles, cumin lamb, spicy oil.
places.rationale: Noodle Run essential. Watching noodles being pulled is half the experience.

places.name: Pho 777
places.must_try: Pho dac biet (special combo) — 20+ varieties on the menu
places.description: 20+ pho varieties on Argyle Street. Real bone broth depth, massive portions.
places.rationale: Noodle Run anchor on Argyle. Step off the Red Line into Little Saigon.

places.name: Okdol Hyeonok
places.must_try: Mul naengmyeon — icy beef broth with buckwheat noodles
places.description: Bib Gourmand Pyongyang-style cold noodles. Buckwheat noodles in icy beef broth.
places.rationale: Noodle Run: Korean edition. Cold noodles that feel like nothing you've had before.

places.name: Boonie's
places.must_try: Sisig — crispy pork face hash, the gateway to Filipino food
places.description: Bib Gourmand next-gen Filipino. Heritage recipes with modern technique.
places.rationale: If Kasama is the splurge, Boonie's is the casual entry point.

places.name: Thattu
places.must_try: Kerala beef fry — NYT-recognized for a reason
places.description: Kerala street food on Devon Ave. Dosas, appam, fish curry, beef fry.
places.rationale: Combine with Sabri Nihari + Momo World for a Devon Ave triple play.

places.name: Carnitas Uruapan
places.must_try: Carnitas by the pound — copper cazo visible from the street
places.description: Cash-only Pilsen institution. Giant copper pot cooking pork all day.
places.rationale: The copper cazo street spectacle + legendary pork. Pair with Pilsen murals.

places.name: McDonald's Global Menu
places.must_try: Whatever global items are rotating — Japan, Spain, India, Australia
places.description: THE global McDonald's at HQ. Standard menu PLUS rotating international items. 6,000 sq ft futuristic restaurant.
places.rationale: She thinks international McDonald's is cool — this IS that. Right off the Eisenhower.

places.name: Zmzm Sweets
places.address: 10511 S Harlem Ave, Chicago Ridge, IL 60415
places.must_try: Assorted baklava box + fresh kunafa (crispy cheese pastry)
places.description: Chicago's premier Middle Eastern/Turkish sweets. Baklava, kunafa, Arabic ice cream (booza).
places.rationale: She loved baklava — Zmzm is the mothership. Ships nationwide for a reason.

places.name: Libanais Sweets
places.must_try: Mixed baklava sampler — 80+ years of family recipes from Beirut
places.description: Family bakery starting in Beirut 1935. Every sweet baked in-house.
places.rationale: Sweet Tooth + Devon Ave day. Baklava as an art form, 3 generations.

places.name: Do-Rite Donuts
places.must_try: Pistachio-Meyer lemon glazed
places.description: Pillowy vanilla bean, cinnamon crunch, Michigan apple fritter. Also fried chicken.
places.rationale: Doughnut trail anchor downtown. Right off the Eisenhower.

places.name: Doughnut Vault
places.must_try: Old fashioned buttermilk — sells out fast, go early
places.description: Small-batch, speakeasy-like vibe. Sells out daily. Old fashioned specialists.
places.rationale: The scarcity IS the experience. If you get there and they're gone, that's the story.

places.name: Old Fashioned Donuts
places.must_try: Apple fritter — #16 on Yelp's Top 100 US Donut Shops
places.description: Nearly 50 years. Every donut hand-cut, fried, and glazed from scratch. LEGEND.
places.rationale: The South Side pilgrimage. 45-min detour but worth a dedicated donut day.

places.name: Stan's Donuts — Oak Brook
places.must_try: Biscoff banana pocket
places.description: LA transplant, now Chicago institution. Inventive flavors.
places.rationale: RIGHT on I-88. Grab one on the way to or from the city.

places.name: Firecakes Donuts
places.must_try: Ice cream donut sandwich (summer only)
places.description: About a dozen flavors. Famous for donut ice cream sandwiches in summer.
places.rationale: Sweet Tooth summer essential. River North, right off I-290.

places.name: Beacon Doughnuts
places.address: 810 W Armitage Ave, Chicago, IL 60614 (alley entrance)
places.must_try: Find the secret alley walk-up window — that's the must-try
places.description: Secret walk-up window in an alley. Small-batch artisanal. The hidden entrance IS the charm.
places.rationale: Curiosity Cabinet meets Sweet Tooth. A secret donut window in an alley.

places.name: Pretty Cool Ice Cream
places.must_try: Whatever pastry chef Dana Cree invented this week
places.description: From award-winning pastry chef Dana Cree. Ice cream bars, sandwiches, pints with inventive flavors.
places.rationale: Trust-the-chef meets Sweet Tooth. A pastry genius making ice cream.

places.name: Sukhadia's Indian Sweets
places.must_try: Fresh jalebi — hot, syrupy, crunchy spirals
places.description: Indian sweet shop on Devon. Fresh jalebi, gulab jamun, barfi, ladoo.
places.rationale: Sweet Tooth on Devon Ave. Hot jalebi from a sweet shop is a life-changing snack.

places.name: Paleteria Arco Iris
places.must_try: Mango con chile paleta — spicy-sweet frozen perfection
places.description: Mexican popsicle shop. Mango con chile, tamarindo, coconut. Plus churros.
places.rationale: Sweet Tooth in Pilsen. Combine with murals and Carnitas Uruapan.

places.name: Naper Nuts & Sweets
places.must_try: Gourmet caramel corn + pick from 4,000 candy varieties
places.description: Since 1983. ~4,000 different candies. Nostalgic candy, chocolates, fudge, gummy everything.
places.rationale: Snack Attack + Sweet Tooth near Glen Ellyn. Downtown Naperville is charming too.

places.name: Mitsuwa Marketplace
places.address: 100 E Algonquin Rd, Arlington Heights, IL 60005
places.must_try: Japanese snack aisle binge + Santouka ramen in food court
places.description: Massive Japanese grocery with food court. Rows of wild snacks, turtle-shaped pastries, ramen, takoyaki.
places.rationale: Snack Attack paradise. The snack aisle alone is a 30-min adventure. She will lose her mind.

places.name: H Mart — Naperville
places.must_try: The snack aisle + whatever the food court is serving today
places.description: Massive Korean/Asian supermarket. In-store food court, insane snack aisle. 15 min from Glen Ellyn.
places.rationale: She loved the Korean supermarket. This one is basically in your backyard.

places.name: Joong Boo Market
places.must_try: Walk the whole kimchi aisle + $30 sashimi platter (Wed/Fri)
places.description: Chicago's premiere Asian grocery since 1992. Famous whole kimchi aisle. Korean food court inside.
places.rationale: Fermentation Station anchor. The kimchi aisle is a religious experience.

places.name: Exotic Snack Guys
places.must_try: Build a mystery box from 40 countries
places.description: Snacks from ~40 countries. Rare chips, candies, beverages you've never seen.
places.rationale: She likes exotic snacks — this is the mothership. Mystery boxes available.

places.name: Candycopia
places.must_try: Build your own Swedish candy bag — pick-n-mix bar
places.description: Swedish pick-and-mix candy bar plus international selections. Two locations on the corridor.
places.rationale: Snack Attack right on I-290. Perfect grab on the drive in or out.

places.name: American Science & Surplus
places.must_try: Find the weirdest thing — beakers, robot kits, surplus army oddities
places.description: THE science bulk store. Surplus lab glass, educational toys, army surplus, electronics, things you didn't know existed. Employee-owned.
places.rationale: 10 min from Glen Ellyn. A tinkerer's wonderland and curiosity cabinet rolled into one.

places.name: Game Show Battle Rooms
places.must_try: The 1-hour 3-game-show competition — bring competitive energy
places.description: Immersive game show competition. Teams face off in 3 different shows over 1 hour.
places.rationale: 5 min from Glen Ellyn! She likes the idea of game shows — this is literally it.

places.name: Woolly Mammoth Antiques & Oddities
places.must_try: Find the jar of old dentures, the insane asylum records, or a taxidermy piece
places.description: THE curiosity cabinet of Chicago. Taxidermy, skulls, medical artifacts, old books, creepy dolls. Taxidermy classes offered.
places.rationale: She likes old things and oddities — Woolly Mammoth IS this category. Middle East Bakery is NEXT DOOR for baklava.

places.name: Middle East Bakery & Grocery
places.must_try: Brick oven pies + grab a baklava box
places.description: Since 1981. Brick oven pies, Middle Eastern grocery, fresh baklava. RIGHT NEXT TO Woolly Mammoth.
places.rationale: Curiosity Cabinet + Sweet Tooth combo. Oddities then baklava, 10 feet apart.

places.name: Salvage One
places.must_try: Get lost in 60,000 sq ft across 3 floors of architectural salvage
places.description: THREE FLOORS of architectural salvage, vintage furniture, oddities. 60,000 sq ft warehouse.
places.rationale: Old things and oddities at warehouse scale. Near the I-290 exit.

places.name: Intuit Art Museum
places.must_try: Henry Darger's apartment recreation — a reclusive janitor's 15,000-page fantasy epic
places.description: Outsider art museum. Henry Darger was a janitor who secretly created a 15,000-page illustrated fantasy. His apartment is recreated here.
places.rationale: Curiosity Cabinet essential. Outsider art is the perfect blend of old, odd, and fascinating.

places.name: International Museum of Surgical Science
places.address: 1524 N Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60610
places.must_try: The trepanning tools and the recreated 19th-century pharmacy
places.description: North America's ONLY surgery museum. In a lakefront mansion modeled after Versailles.
places.rationale: She likes oddities — a mansion full of surgical history is peak curiosity cabinet.

places.name: Willowbrook Wildlife Center
places.address: 525 S Park Blvd, Glen Ellyn, IL 60137
places.must_try: Find the resident Great Horned Owls — her special interest
places.description: Wildlife rehab with resident owls and raptors. Free outdoor exhibits, nature trail. IN Glen Ellyn.
places.rationale: She has an owl special interest. There are owls HERE. In your base. Free.

places.name: Pilsen Mural Walk + National Museum of Mexican Art
places.must_try: The museum is always free — don't skip it after the murals
places.description: 50+ murals in Pilsen's streets + world-class free museum of Mexican art.
places.rationale: One of America's greatest outdoor galleries. Pair with Carnitas Uruapan or HaiSous.

places.name: The 606 / Bloomingdale Trail
places.must_try: Walk west to east and drop into Wicker Park or Logan Square for food
places.description: 2.7-mile elevated rail trail through 4 of Chicago's coolest neighborhoods.
places.rationale: Chicago's High Line but less crowded. Free, beautiful, great people-watching.

places.name: Garfield Park Conservatory
places.address: 300 N Central Park Ave, Chicago, IL 60624
places.must_try: The palm house is jaw-dropping — then the desert room
places.description: One of the nation's largest conservatories. Palm house, fern room, desert room. Free.
places.rationale: Free, gorgeous, and never on tourist lists. Great morning stop before the 606.

places.name: Bronzeville Heritage Walk
places.must_try: Victory Monument + DuSable Museum (nearby)
places.description: Historic 'Black Metropolis' — Great Migration, Louis Armstrong, birth of Chicago blues.
places.rationale: Deep American history in these streets. Pair with DuSable Museum.

places.name: Chicago Riverwalk Kayaking
places.must_try: Morning slot for calmer water — paddle past Marina City
places.description: Kayak through the skyscraper canyon. Guided architecture tours from the water.
places.rationale: Architecture from the water, but YOU'RE paddling. Active and unique.

places.name: Maxwell Street Market
places.must_try: Maxwell Street Polish sausage + elote from the street vendors
places.description: Sunday open-air market since 1912. Street food, vintage, produce.
places.rationale: Weekend Warriors essential. The food stalls alone are worth the trip.

places.name: Eataly Chicago
places.must_try: Graze: cheese sample + slice + gelato + Nutella bar
places.description: 63,000 sq ft on TWO FLOORS. Largest Eataly in the US. Multiple restaurants, Nutella bar, gelato.
places.rationale: The ultimate grazing destination. Wander, sample, eat, repeat.

places.name: The Sterling Food Hall
places.must_try: Graze across 13 stalls — Fat Shallot sandwiches + LaShuk Middle Eastern
places.description: 24,000 sq ft in a 1907 Burnham building. ~13 diverse stalls.
places.rationale: Bite-here-and-there meal in the Loop. Right off the Eisenhower.

places.name: Glen Ellyn Antique District
places.must_try: Walk the full cluster: Sign of the Whale, Gather & Collect, 30 North, Twice Is Nice
places.description: 8+ walkable antique/vintage/thrift shops in downtown Glen Ellyn.
places.rationale: She likes old things — and these are at HOME BASE. Lazy afternoon browse.

places.name: Graham Crackers Comics
places.must_try: Hunt for Krang figures and vintage TMNT collectibles
places.description: Largest comic chain in America. TMNT back issues, collectibles, new releases.
places.rationale: Krang special interest — this is where to hunt. Also has suburban locations near Glen Ellyn.

places.name: Weird Chicago Tour
places.must_try: The bus tour of haunted, bizarre, and macabre Chicago
places.description: Bus tour of Chicago's most haunted, bizarre, and macabre sites.
places.rationale: Curiosity Cabinet on wheels. Ghost stories, crime scenes, oddities.

places.name: Chicago Pedway Underground Tour
places.must_try: The stained glass and underground swimming pool views
places.description: 2-hour walking tour through Chicago's hidden underground passages.
places.rationale: Hidden passages under the Loop. Oddities meets architecture.

places.name: Reckless Records
places.must_try: Dig through the vinyl — Jack White has played in-store shows here
places.description: Chicago institution since 1988. Massive vinyl selection, staff picks wall.
places.rationale: Old things + oddities in record form. Wicker Park, combinable with the 606.

places.name: Vargo Brothers Ferments
places.must_try: Kimchi-kraut hybrid — Black-owned, artisan fermentation
places.description: Black-owned fermentation company. Kimchi, kraut, kombucha at farmers markets.
places.rationale: Fermentation Station essential. Find them at Green City Market or Logan Square market.

places.name: Beautiful Rind
places.must_try: Ask for a tasting of the funkiest cheese they have
places.description: Artisan cheese shop with classes. Funky aged cheeses, cheese boards, expert staff.
places.rationale: Fermentation Station: cheese edition. Ask for the weirdest thing.

places.name: Lincoln Park Zoo
places.must_try: Birds of Prey area — eagles, owls, and vultures up close
places.description: Free zoo. Birds of Prey area has owls. Also great for a general visit.
places.rationale: Free + owls. Can't beat that combo.

places.name: Argyle Street / Little Saigon
places.must_try: Step off the Red Line at Argyle — the gateway arch marks the start
places.description: Vietnamese corridor. Pho shops, banh mi bakeries, tea houses, Asian groceries.
places.rationale: CTA accessible. Combine with Demera for an Ethiopian-Vietnamese double feature.

places.name: Devon Avenue South Asian Corridor
places.must_try: Walk the full mile — sari shops, Bollywood music, sweet shop jalebi
places.description: A mile of Indian, Pakistani, Nepali shops, restaurants, and groceries. Sari shops, Bollywood music stores.
places.rationale: Full sensory immersion. Eat at Sabri Nihari + Thattu + Momo World + Sukhadia's sweets.
